mission.title: thanos: e2e: Moved to Cortex e2e test and GithubActions.
mission.description:
This PR moves all Thanos e2e tests to the new [Cortex e2e framework](https://github.com/cortexproject/cortex/tree/master/integration/e2e) written by amazing @pracucci . 

Our e2e tests are responsible for spinning Thanos, Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Minio components by executing pre-built binary and invoking some more or less complex tests. 

### Why changing?

Previously our e2e tests were using our written scheduler which schedules all components as children processes. It was also running on CircleCI inside docker(!). This was problematic for various reasons:

* It was harder to clean up on crash.
* Process isolation was weaker: We couldn't isolate resources (e.g network: everything could talk to everything). This also mean measuring performance was harder. 
* It was highly unreliable/flaky, mostly due to CircleCI free tier being ultra slow.

Fixes: https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/issues/2046
Fixes: https://github.com/thanos-io/thanos/issues/2045
Partially: https://github.com/t

resolution.summary:
* New code also allows the different implementation of service (e.g you will see why it's needed for Thanos).
* Make more things type safe now instead of fragile handwritten strings as references.
* Also added customi
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
